questionHtml: The molecular structures of the mouse alarm compound and fox scent are very similar. Suggest an evolutionary advantage for this chemical similarity.
markschemeHtml:
the mice would have the same response to another mouse’s danger signal as to the actual presence of the predator/fox
OR
adaptation to fool predator by producing a scent similar to predator’s own scent
OR
allows a group response to a predator/fox/danger when just one mouse detects the danger ✔

Accept any other feasible answer.

meta.title: The molecular structures of the mouse alarm compound and fox scent are very similar. Suggest an evolutionary advantage for this chemical similarity.
